stories.text: As an application developer, I want committed data to survive a process crash so that I do not need to implement my own durability layer.
As an application developer, I want the database to recover automatically on restart so that I do not need to write recovery logic or run repair tools.
As an application developer, I want to choose the fsync policy (per-write, batched, commit-only) so that I can trade latency for durability based on my workload.

personas.name: Aion Engine
personas.role: Relying on haematite for workflow event durability

stories.text: As the Aion engine, I want acknowledged event appends to be durable so that a workflow crash does not lose events the recorder believes are persisted.
As the Aion engine, I want crash recovery to replay uncommitted WAL entries so that events appended but not yet tree-committed are not lost.
As the Aion engine, I want each shard to recover independently so that a slow-recovering shard does not block workflows on other shards.
As an operations engineer, I want corrupted WAL entries to be detected and skipped during recovery so that a partial disk failure does not prevent the database from starting.
As an operations engineer, I want the disk node store to handle millions of nodes without filesystem performance degradation so that I do not need to tune directory structures.
As an operations engineer, I want the LRU cache size to be configurable so that I can tune memory usage based on available RAM and access patterns.
As an operations engineer, I want recovery to log what it replayed so that I can audit what happened during an unclean shutdown.
